seoTitle: Burnley vs Burnside Heights — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Burnley and Burnside Heights. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Burnley vs Burnside Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burnley and Burnside Heights.
heading: Burnley vs Burnside Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burnley, Victoria and Burnside Heights, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burnside Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Burnley (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burnley or Burnside Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burnley has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Burnside Heights scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burnley scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Burnside Heights scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burnley or Burnside Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burnside Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
